what_it_does: This file is a build-order refresh for the recollection / episodic-memory loop-prep work: it records the current built state of an eight-stage pipeline, identifies the remaining gaps, lists Company components to reuse, sequences dependency-ordered build beats, states standing disciplines, and reframes the work as building into the Company centre rather than as a separate recollection island.
observations: Opens with a quoted Tim directive dated 2026-06-17 and declares itself a refresh of earlier Jun-14/15 loop-prep docs.
Contains an eight-stage current-state table (CAPTURE, DISTILL, EMBED, LINK, GATHER, JUDGE, RECALL, HEALTH) with per-stage 'exists now', state emoji, and 'the gap to build'.
Identifies JUDGE as the apex gap: verdicts+atoms schema exists but has zero writers and the judgment layer is absent/stub.

Includes a 'REUSE-DON'T-REPEAT' section mapping recollection needs onto Company components: find_relations, relation_types, forms/lifters, common_knowledge, corpus_rerank, /api/cognition/neighbours, session_recall, SUBSTRATE-ARCHITECTURE.
Lists seven dependency-ordered build beats (DISTILL real, EMBED multi-lens, LINK semantic, JUDGE, GATHER full, RECALL proactive, HEALTH) plus a cross-cutting union seam/addressing beat.

States standing disciplines: registry-is-truth, no-hardcoding, conversation-is-source-of-truth, four root axes, verification-state, function+form, commit-to-main, no-versioning, reuse-don't-parallel, build-more-than-planned, scout-before-build, co-scope file-disjoint propose-diff.
Declares 'OPEN DECISIONS: ZERO' and says build-order is the only open choice, to be adjusted by Tim.

Section F introduces an 'INTO-THE-CENTRE REFRAME' law: the Company is the single centre; recollection's good parts are absorbed into it and recollection sheds parallel scaffolding to ride the Company spine.
Ends with an explicit symbols list: symbols[] = [].
standouts: The apex gap is named as the JUDGE layer, described as the keystone that gates autonomy.
The 'into-the-centre' reframe supersedes the earlier island-build plan and redirects all work into the Company core.
A large portion of the document is a reuse inventory, arguing against parallel scaffolding.
The build beats are explicitly dependency-ordered and each includes a 'build-more' expansion.
conventions: Uses hierarchical lettered sections (A-F), tables, and bulleted lists.
Uses emoji/state markers (✅, ◑, ○) for build status.
Cites specific file/line references (suite.py:10854, mcp_face:918) and prior session identifiers (R1-R10, D-1..D-15).
Follows a 'no branches / no versioning / update in place' documentation style.
Breaks typical local-project planning by explicitly declaring that the local project should be dissolved into a shared centre.
concerns: Heavy reliance on external Company modules and prior session identifiers that are not defined inside this file.
Dense jargon and acronyms (P10, P5, 4B, D-10, R9, etc.) make the file hard to parse without external context.
The 'ZERO open decisions' claim sits next to a note that build-order is still open and requires Tim adjustment.
The 'into-the-centre' reframe may invalidate earlier local decisions but does not specify migration steps or ownership handoff.
Symbols list is empty even though the text references many code symbols.
notes: A dense, directive-heavy planning doc that maps what is built, what is missing, and what to reuse, while reframing the whole effort as absorption into the Company centre.
questions: What do the identifiers P10, P5, P2, P6, P3, P4, P7/P8, P8, R1-R10, D-1..D-15, and D-10 refer to exactly?
What is the 'served-4B' model/service and where is it defined?
What are the four seam-wires mentioned in seam-mapping.md?
What is the current physical/data boundary between recollection and the Company corpus?
What is 'union-build/#71/legibility' and how does it affect sequencing?
How will recollection 'shed' its sqlite-vec store, MCP server, and embed client without losing existing data?
What does 'no-versioning' mean for a markdown file that is itself a refresh of prior docs?
What is the exact ratification process with Tim for the JUDGE gate?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to refresh the stale build order and line up the full scope; the actual content does that and additionally imposes a strong centralizing reframe that shifts where the work is built.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to prevent the recollection subsystem from becoming a permanent parallel island, to sequence its remaining work, and to justify folding its good design into the shared Company core so the Company spine improves and recollection rides it.
novelty: The explicit 'into-the-centre' law that an island's good parts must be absorbed into the company centre and the island must then shed its own scaffolding is unusual for a build-order doc; the eight-stage current-state/gap table is also a distinct structural choice.
contribution: It contributes a current-state/gap map, a dependency-ordered build plan, a reuse inventory against existing Company components, a centralization principle for absorbing subsystem design, and a set of cross-cutting disciplines for building recall capabilities into a shared spine.
summary_for_embedding: A build-order refresh document that maps the current state and gaps of an eight-stage recollection pipeline, sequences reuse-heavy build beats, and reframes the work as absorption into the Company centre rather than a separate island.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to align the remaining recollection build work with Tim's directive by lining up the full scope, prioritizing reuse of Company components, and redirecting construction into the shared Company core.
